# Pseudo-translate to catch UI truncation early

> Generated from [`walkthroughs/kapi-pseudo-translate.md`](https://github.com/neokapi/neokapi/blob/main/web/docs/walkthroughs/kapi-pseudo-translate.md). Do not edit by hand — change the prompt and regenerate.

You're shipping a feature with new strings and want to catch UI truncation problems _before_ the strings come back from a real translation provider. Pseudo-translation expands every string with locale-shaped accent characters so issues surface immediately on screen — no API key, no waiting, no cost.

## Scene 1 — pseudo-translate

<ThemedVideo
  sources={{
    light: "/video/kapi/01-pseudo-translate.webm",
    dark: "/video/kapi/01-pseudo-translate.webm",
  }}
/>

Open a JSON message catalog (`messages.json`), run `kapi pseudo-translate ... --target-lang fr` to generate a French-shaped pseudo-translation, and inspect the output JSON to confirm the expansion.

Pseudo-translation expands every string with diacritical characters so truncation, clipping, or missing-string bugs are immediately visible when the UI re-renders. It's part of the development loop, not the translation pipeline.

## Next

For deeper QA, see [Quality checks](/features/qa-checks). To run pseudo-translation in the same flow as a TM-leverage step, see [`kapi run`](/kapi-cli/commands/flow).
